From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/33/NGC2207%2BIC2163.jpg/330px-NGC2207%2BIC2163.jpg)](/wiki/File:NGC2207%2BIC2163.jpg)

NGC 2207 tidally stripping IC 2163.

**Tidal stripping** occurs when a larger [galaxy](/wiki/Galaxy "Galaxy") pulls [stars](/wiki/Star "Star") and other stellar material from a smaller galaxy because of strong [tidal forces](/wiki/Tidal_force "Tidal force").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

An example of this scenario is the [interacting](/wiki/Interacting_galaxy "Interacting galaxy") pair of galaxies [NGC 2207 and IC 2163](/wiki/NGC_2207_and_IC_2163 "NGC 2207 and IC 2163"), which are currently in the process of tidal stripping.
